pg135-p1-s1: Whether the family goes on a spiritual basis or not, the alcoholic member has to if he would recover.
pg135-p1-s2:
The others must be
convinced of his new status beyond the shadow of a doubt.
pg135-p1-s3:
Seeing is believing to most families who have lived with a
drinker.
pg135-p1:
Whether the family goes on a spiritual basis or not, the alcoholic member has to if he would recover. The others must be
convinced of his new status beyond the shadow of a doubt. Seeing is believing to most families who have lived with a
drinker.
pg135-p2-s1: Here is a case in point: One of our friends is a heavy smoker and coffee drinker.
pg135-p2-s2: There was no doubt he over-indulged.
pg135-p2-s3: Seeing this, and meaning to be helpful, his wife commenced to admonish him about it.
pg135-p2-s4:
He admitted he was overdoing these
things, but frankly said that he was not ready to stop.
pg135-p2-s5:
His wife is one of those persons who really feels there is
something rather sinful about these commodities, so she nagged, and her intolerance finally threw him into a fit of
anger.
pg135-p2:
Here is a case in point: One of our friends is a heavy smoker and coffee drinker. There was no doubt he over-indulged. Seeing this, and meaning to be helpful, his wife commenced to admonish him about it. He admitted he was overdoing these
things, but frankly said that he was not ready to stop. His wife is one of those persons who really feels there is
something rather sinful about these commodities, so she nagged, and her intolerance finally threw him into a fit of
anger. He got drunk.
pg135-p3-s1: Of course our friend was wrong - dead wrong.
pg135-p3-s2: He had to painfully admit that and mend his spiritual fences.
pg135-p3-s3:
Though he is
now a most effective member of Alcoholics Anonymous, he still smokes and drinks coffee, but neither his wife nor anyone
else stands in judgment.
pg135-p3-s4:
She sees she was wrong to make a burning issue out of such a matter when his more serious
ailments were being rapidly cured.
pg135-p3:
Of course our friend was wrong - dead wrong. He had to painfully admit that and mend his spiritual fences. Though he is
now a most effective member of Alcoholics Anonymous, he still smokes and drinks coffee, but neither his wife nor anyone
else stands in judgment. She sees she was wrong to make a burning issue out of such a matter when his more serious
ailments were being rapidly cured.
pg135-p4-s1: We have three little mottoes which are apropos.
